description: Single filer, age 67 (born 1958), one W-2 with $80,000 wages and $5,000 box7 social security tips (also declared as qualified tips on Schedule 1-A), $10,000 federal withheld, standard deduction, no dependents. Exercises BOTH OBBBA below-the-line deductions (senior deduction + Schedule 1-A tips) AND the §63(f) age-65 additional standard deduction in a single compute() call AND the wave-3-cleanup validation pipeline. The OBBBA Schedule 1-A deductions land on Form 1040 line 13b (taxable income only), NOT on Schedule 1 (AGI is unchanged at $80,000). The return is FFFF-compatible — no blockers triggered.
authority: skill/reference/ty2025-constants.json: OBBBA standard deduction $15,750 (single); §63(f) age-65/blind additional $2,000 (single/HoH)
skill/scripts/calc/patches/obbba_senior_deduction.py: single base $6,000, phase-out starts at $75,000, 6% rate
skill/scripts/calc/patches/obbba_schedule_1a.py: tips cap $25,000, single phase-out starts at $150,000, no phase-out at MAGI $80k
IRS 2025 Schedule 1-A: senior + tips + overtime total flows to Form 1040 line 13b (below AGI; reduces taxable income only, not AGI/MAGI)
IRS 2025 Form 1040: line 14 = line 12 + line 13a + line 13b; line 15 (taxable income) = line 11 (AGI) - line 14
CP4 tenforty verification locked Single TY2025 brackets for federal income tax

hand_check.age_65_additional_std_ded: single/HoH $2,000 per box; taxpayer is 65+ -> 1 box -> $2,000 (this is the long-standing §63(f) additional standard deduction, distinct from the OBBBA senior deduction)
hand_check.senior_deduction_calc: base 1 * $6000 = $6000; MAGI = AGI $80000 (the OBBBA deduction does NOT reduce its own MAGI); phase-out excess $80000 - $75000 = $5000; reduction 6% * $5000 = $300; final $6000 - $300 = $5700
hand_check.adjustments_total: 0 (OBBBA deductions are NOT Schedule 1 Part II adjustments)
hand_check.agi_calc: 80000 total income - 0 adjustments = 80000 (OBBBA deductions do not touch AGI)
hand_check.tax_tables_delta: IRS tax tables use $50 midpoints; tenforty rounds to 6261 (+6 vs bracket formula — same mechanism as simple_w2_standard)
